subject: Hausa
question_text: How many letters are in the Hausa alphabet (boko)?
explanation: Hausa uses 29 letters including ɓ, ɗ, ƙ, ƴ, and ' (glottal stop).

subject: Hausa
question_text: What is the Hausa word for 'God'?
explanation: Allah (Arabic), Ubangiji (Lord), and Maɗaukaki (The Most High) all refer to God in Hausa.

subject: Hausa
option_a: Good morning (How did you spend the night?)
explanation: Ina kwana? = Good morning. Response: Lahiya lau (Fine/Peace).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Ruwa means water. Wuta is fire, ƙasa is earth/ground, iska is air/wind.

subject: Hausa
question_text: Who is considered the father of modern Hausa literature?
explanation: Abubakar Imam wrote 'Ruwan Bagaja' and pioneered modern Hausa prose. Nana Asmau wrote poetry earlier.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Na gode = Thank you (I thank). Mungode = We thank you.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Numbers: Ɗaya (1), Biyu (2), Uku (3), Huɗu (4), Biyar (5).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Sannu is a common greeting. Sannunku is plural/respectful form.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Namiji means man (male). Mutum means person/human, mace means woman, yaro means boy.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Yaya kake? = How are you (male). Yaya kike? = How are you (female).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Gida means house/home. Ƙofa is door, taga is window, daki is room.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Don Allah = Please (literally 'for God'). Polite request formula.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Ci means eat. Sha means drink, kwanciya means lie down/sleep, tafi means go.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Eh means yes. A'a means no.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Mace means woman. Namiji is man, yarinya is girl, uwa is mother.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Sai an jima = See you later/Until later. Sai gobe = See you tomorrow.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Kasuwa means market. Gida is house, hanya is road, gari is town.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Ina son ka = I like/love you (male). Ina son ki = I love you (female).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Sarki means king/chief. Hakimi is district head, waziri is vizier/advisor.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Uba means father. Uwa is mother, ɗa is son/child, ƴa is daughter.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Rana means sun/day. Wata is moon/month, tauraro is star, sama is sky/heaven.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Barka da asuba = Good morning. Barka da yamma = Good afternoon. Barka da dare = Good night.

subject: Hausa
option_d: All mean child depending on gender
explanation: Yaro means child/boy, ɗa means son, ƴa means daughter.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Kai = you (male). Ke = you (female). Ku = you (plural).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Zo means come. Tafi means go, zauna means sit, tashi means stand/get up.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Lafiya means health/peace. Laifiya lau = Fine/Well (response to greetings).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Abinci means food. Ruwa is water, nama is meat, tuwo is staple porridge.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Mene ne sunanka? = What is your name (male). Sunanki (female).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Numbers: Biyar (5), Shida (6), Bakwai (7), Takwas (8), Tara (9).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Yi hakuri = Sorry/Have patience. Used as apology or to calm someone.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Saniya means cow. Rago is ram, akuya is goat, kaza is chicken.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Madalla = Excellent/Wonderful. Expression of admiration or approval.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Makaranta means school. Masallaci is mosque, asibiti is hospital, kasuwa is market.

subject: Hausa
question_text: The traditional Hausa leader of a town is called:
explanation: Sarki is king/chief, Mai is ruler (Maiduguri), Dagaci is village head.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Allah ya kiyaye = May God protect. Common blessing/prayer.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Littafi means book. Takarda is paper, alƙalami is pen, rubutu is writing.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Toh = Okay/Alright/Well. Common discourse marker.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Itace means tree/wood. Ciyawa is grass, daji is forest/bush, fure is flower.

subject: Hausa
question_text: The Hausa name for God as 'The King' is:
explanation: Sarkin Sarakuna = King of Kings. Common epithet for God in Hausa Islamic poetry.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Nagari = Good/Fine. Ba nagari ba = Not good/Bad.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Kifi means fish. Nama is meat, kaza is chicken, tsuntsu is bird.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Zan tafi = I will go. Ina tafiya = I am going. Na tafi = I went.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Uwa means mother. Uba is father, ɗa is son, ƴa is daughter.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Sai gobe = Until tomorrow/See you tomorrow. Sai wata rana = See you another day.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Dare means night. Rana is day/sun, yamma is evening/afternoon, safe is morning.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Maraba = Welcome. Sannu da zuwa = Welcome (greeting to arrival).

subject: Hausa
explanation: Aboki means friend. Maƙwabci is neighbor, ɗan'uwa is relative/brother, bako is stranger/guest.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Na gaji = I am tired. Ina jin yunwa = I am hungry. Ina jin ƙishirwa = I am thirsty.

subject: Hausa
question_text: The Hausa proverb 'Komi nisan dare, gari zai waye' means:
option_a: No matter how long the night, dawn will break
explanation: This proverb teaches hope and patience: difficult times will eventually pass.

subject: Hausa
explanation: Bismillah (Bismillahir Rahmanir Rahim) = In the name of God, the Most Gracious, Most Merciful. Said before starting anything.
